view_matching_logic.Plan Section: Validate this Countertop Above rule only for Plan Section Base views where the cut plane passes through lower/base cabinet geometry and the countertop exists above the cut plane. Plan Section Wall views and views containing INDICATES COUNTERTOP BELOW shall be omitted for this rule.

geometry_logic.boundary_detection: The rule is activated only when the detected view is a Plan Section Base showing lower/base cabinet geometry where the countertop exists above the cut plane. All other Plan Section types shall be omitted. For this rule, a detected PLAN SECTION BASE view itself is a strong activation condition. Do not require visible base cabinet boxes, sink base, drawer layout, or knee space. If the view title is PLAN SECTION BASE and the view shows a counter/countertop run, countertop edge/profile, plywood/subtop, counter support, glazing counter condition, or long countertop-related plan-section geometry, the countertop-above condition is active. If the required INDICATES COUNTERTOP ABOVE note is missing, return FAIL. Activate this rule only when the detected view is a PLAN SECTION BASE and the same view contains clear evidence that a countertop exists, such as an explicit countertop callout, countertop profile/outline, or countertop-related annotation. A PLAN SECTION BASE title alone shall not activate this rule. If no countertop evidence is visible in the same view, the rule shall be OMITTED.
wall_interaction_conditions: Plan Section title or geometry shows base/lower cabinet casework rather than upper/wall cabinet only.
Visible plan-section contains lower cabinet boxes, partitions, sink base, drawer/door layout, knee space, or support below countertop.
Countertop is implied above the cut plane and is not the primary visible plan surface.
The note should be located inside or near the lower/base plan-section view, often near the cabinet footprint.
Do not activate where the view shows wall/upper cabinet plan-section above the counter; that belongs to countertop below.
Do not activate where the countertop itself is fully visible in a normal plan or elevation/section.
Do not activate for vertical Section/Detail views showing countertop edge, support, or finished floor.
The detected view represents a Plan Section Base.
The visible geometry corresponds to lower/base cabinet casework below the countertop plane.
If both ABOVE and BELOW text appear near different views on the same sheet, validate only the note belonging to the exact view crop.
geometry_logic.expected_boundary_region: The expected region is the base/lower cabinet Plan Section crop: inside or immediately around lower cabinet footprints, partitions, sink base, knee space, counter support below, cabinet depth/width dimensions, and the note area pointing to or overlaying the lower casework where countertop exists above. The detected note must be in the same Plan Section view_id.

pass_condition: The exact detected view is a Plan Section showing lower/base cabinet geometry below a countertop plane.
The countertop-above condition is active in that same view and not inferred from another Plan Section.
The required note or accepted variant is visibly present in that same view near the base/lower cabinet plan-section region.
Detected note evidence, detected_text, and coordinates are non-empty and belong to the same view_id/crop.
Confidence score is greater than or equal to 75.
fail_condition: The exact Plan Section clearly shows lower/base cabinet geometry where countertop is above the cut plane.
The required countertop-above note is missing from that same view while the applicable lower cabinet region is clearly visible.
Failure is not allowed for Plan Section type alone; the lower/base cabinet below-counter cut condition must be active.
Failure is allowed only when explicit countertop evidence is present in the same Plan Section Base view and the required INDICATES COUNTERTOP ABOVE note is missing.

The detected view is PLAN SECTION BASE and shows a counter/countertop run, countertop edge/profile, plywood/subtop, counter support, or countertop-related plan-section geometry, but the required INDICATES COUNTERTOP ABOVE note is missing.
Failure is allowed only when the detected view is a Plan Section Base / base-lower cabinet countertop-above condition and does not contain any COUNTERTOP BELOW note.
review_required_condition: The Plan Section crop is unclear, clipped, or rotated and the cut level cannot be determined.
It is unclear whether the visible cabinet geometry is lower/base cabinet below countertop or upper/wall cabinet above countertop.
Text is partially readable and could be ABOVE or BELOW.
Both countertop above and countertop below notes appear nearby and association to the exact view is ambiguous.
The note is detected but coordinates do not clearly fall inside the same Plan Section crop.
omit_condition: The detected view is not a Plan Section.
The Plan Section does not show lower/base cabinet geometry below a countertop plane.
The view shows upper/wall/tall cabinet plan-section geometry where countertop is below the cut plane.
The view is a normal Plan, Elevation, Section, Detail, schedule, or general note area.
The countertop is directly visible in elevation/section/detail and does not require an above indication.
The view shows door, finished floor, in-wall strapping, wall finish, support, or floor condition without lower/base countertop-above plan-section geometry.
The detected view is not a Plan Section Base.
The detected view is not PLAN SECTION BASE and does not clearly represent a base/countertop-above plan-section condition.
The detected view is a Plan Section Wall view; Countertop Above validation is not applicable.
The detected view contains INDICATES COUNTERTOP BELOW or COUNTERTOP BELOW text; this Countertop Above rule shall be OMITTED and shall never FAIL.
The detected view is a standard Plan Section, Plan Section Wall, or any other Plan Section type that does not represent a base cabinet plan section.
The visible geometry does not correspond to lower/base cabinet casework below the countertop plane.
The note appears in another plan section on the same sheet but not in this exact view.

reasoning.description: Explain whether the exact Plan Section shows lower/base cabinet geometry below the countertop plane, whether the countertop-above note was found in that same view, and why similar Plan Sections with upper/wall/tall cabinet geometry are omitted.

view_results.view_type: Detected actual view title from drawing
